user_query: I have germline exome variants from a patient with a suspected rare Mendelian disorder. I want to identify potentially pathogenic coding variants for clinical interpretation.

most_severe.note: Need full per-transcript detail for clinical review

summary.note: Need detailed annotations, not a summary

justification: Clinical rare-disease interpretation of coding variants needs gene/HGVS/protein identifiers, MANE + canonical transcripts for consistent reporting, missense pathogenicity prediction (SIFT, PolyPhen, CADD), ClinVar clinical significance via co-located known variants, and gnomAD exome+genome frequencies to filter out common variants. Per-transcript detail must be preserved (no most_severe/summary).

user_query: A rare disease patient has a variant near an exon-intron boundary. I want to assess whether it disrupts splicing as part of the diagnostic workup.

most_severe.note: Need per-transcript detail to localise the splice effect

justification: A splice-region variant in a diagnostic context needs a dedicated splice predictor (SpliceAI is the current standard), exon/intron numbering to localise the variant relative to the boundary, HGVS/MANE/canonical for reporting, ClinVar significance, and gnomAD frequencies to assess rarity. (MaxEntScan and dbscSNV are reasonable additional splice predictors — left out here to keep the example to high-confidence choices.)

user_query: I'm analysing somatic variants from tumour whole-exome sequencing of cancer patients, called with Mutect2. What VEP options should I use?

most_severe.note: Keep per-transcript detail for driver interpretation

justification: Somatic variant analysis benefits from flagging known co-located variants (dbSNP/COSMIC via check_existing), ClinVar significance, missense pathogenicity prediction (SIFT/PolyPhen/CADD) to prioritise candidate drivers, and gnomAD exome+genome frequencies to filter out residual germline variants. Per-transcript detail is preserved.

user_query: My GWAS hits are mostly in intergenic and intronic regions. I want to understand their potential regulatory effects.
justification: Non-coding/regulatory variants need regulatory-feature consequences (the Ensembl regulatory build), transcript biotype context, co-located known variants and gnomAD frequency. Missense predictors (SIFT/PolyPhen/CADD) do not apply to non-coding variants. (Plugins such as Enformer/UTRAnnotator could add regulatory-impact signal but are left out as a lower-confidence choice.)

user_query: I want to annotate a large cohort VCF with population allele frequencies to compare variant frequencies across populations.

sift.note: Pathogenicity prediction is not the focus

justification: Population-frequency annotation centres on co-located known variants and multi-source allele frequencies: 1000 Genomes global (af) and continental (af_1kg), plus gnomAD exomes (af_gnomade) and genomes (af_gnomadg). Pathogenicity prediction is secondary for this use case.

user_query: We performed CRISPR knockouts in mouse embryonic stem cells and called variants from WGS against the GRCm39 reference. What VEP settings should I use?
justification: For a non-human species (mouse/GRCm39) the human-only resources — PolyPhen, CADD, ClinVar, gnomAD, REVEL, AlphaMissense — are unavailable and must not be enabled. Use species-agnostic annotation: gene symbol, transcript biotype, HGVS, exon/intron numbers, co-located known variants (mouse has variation data), and SIFT (computed by Ensembl for mouse). This example doubles as the canonical test of the species-restriction safety layer.

user_query: I just want to look up a single variant by its rsID — its gene, consequence, and clinical significance.
justification: A quick single-variant lookup needs only the gene symbol, co-located known-variant lookup (to resolve the rsID), ClinVar clinical significance, and HGVS nomenclature. Heavy predictors and frequency panels are unnecessary overhead for a one-off lookup.

user_query: We sequenced a parent-child trio (exome) to find de novo variants causing a paediatric disorder. Which VEP options for clinical interpretation?

most_severe.note: Need per-transcript detail, not a collapsed summary

summary.note: Need per-transcript detail, not a collapsed summary

justification: Trio/de-novo clinical interpretation needs the full coding suite (MANE/canonical, HGVS/protein, SIFT/PolyPhen/CADD, ClinVar), gnomAD frequencies to confirm de-novo rarity, and gene-phenotype associations.

user_query: Tumor-only WES with no matched normal. I need to annotate somatic candidates and aggressively filter likely germline variants by population frequency.

most_severe.note: Need per-transcript detail, not a collapsed summary

summary.note: Need per-transcript detail, not a collapsed summary

justification: Tumor-only analysis leans heavily on population frequency (1000G global + gnomAD exomes/genomes) to subtract germline variants, plus known-variant flagging and pathogenicity prediction for driver candidates.

user_query: ctDNA / liquid-biopsy somatic variants from a cancer patient, low VAF. Annotate for clinical actionability.

most_severe.note: Need per-transcript detail, not a collapsed summary

summary.note: Need per-transcript detail, not a collapsed summary

justification: Liquid-biopsy somatic interpretation focuses on clinical actionability (ClinVar, known variants) and pathogenicity, with gnomAD frequencies to flag germline contamination.

user_query: Variants in promoter/enhancer regions from ATAC-seq peaks - which target genes and regulatory features do they hit?

sift.note: Missense predictors do not apply to non-coding variants

polyphen.note: Missense predictors do not apply to non-coding variants

most_severe.note: Need per-transcript detail, not a collapsed summary

justification: Promoter/enhancer variants need regulatory-feature consequences and up/downstream gene distance to assign target genes; missense predictors are not applicable.

user_query: Do my non-coding GWAS SNPs overlap transcription factor binding sites or other regulatory features?

sift.note: Missense predictors do not apply to non-coding variants

polyphen.note: Missense predictors do not apply to non-coding variants

most_severe.note: Need per-transcript detail, not a collapsed summary

justification: TFBS/regulatory overlap is reported by the regulatory build; missense predictors are not applicable to non-coding variants.

user_query: Comparing variant allele frequencies across continental populations in a cohort. Which frequency annotations should I turn on?
justification: Cross-population frequency comparison uses the full frequency panel: 1000 Genomes global (af) and continental (af_1kg) plus gnomAD exomes/genomes.

user_query: Annotating a very large WGS cohort VCF (~2M variants) is painfully slow. How do I keep it efficient while still getting gene and frequency annotation?

cadd.note: Plugin annotation is slow on millions of variants

spliceai.note: Expensive plugin; skip for cohort-scale runtime

justification: For cohort-scale runtime, keep annotation lean: core gene + gnomAD frequency, and --pick to emit one consequence per variant; skip expensive prediction plugins that dominate runtime.

user_query: Large deletions and duplications from long-read WGS (Sniffles). Which genes and regulatory features are disrupted, and how do I filter common SVs?

sift.note: Missense predictors do not apply to structural variants

polyphen.note: Missense predictors do not apply to structural variants

cadd.note: Missense predictors do not apply to structural variants

most_severe.note: Need per-transcript detail, not a collapsed summary

justification: Structural variants are interpreted by which genes/biotypes and regulatory features they overlap, with gnomAD-SV frequency to remove common SVs; per-base missense predictors do not apply.

user_query: CNV calls from a SNP array - which genes do the copy-number gains/losses affect, and are they common in the population?

sift.note: Missense predictors do not apply to structural variants

polyphen.note: Missense predictors do not apply to structural variants

cadd.note: Missense predictors do not apply to structural variants

most_severe.note: Need per-transcript detail, not a collapsed summary

justification: CNV interpretation centres on overlapped genes/biotypes and gnomAD-SV population frequency; missense predictors are not applicable.

user_query: Clinically interpret structural variants (dels/dups) from a rare-disease WGS case - pathogenic vs benign, filter common ones.

sift.note: Missense predictors do not apply to structural variants

polyphen.note: Missense predictors do not apply to structural variants

most_severe.note: Need per-transcript detail, not a collapsed summary

justification: Clinical SV interpretation combines overlapped-gene context, ClinVar significance via co-located variants, and gnomAD-SV frequency to filter common benign SVs.

user_query: Zebrafish (Danio rerio) variants from a mutagenesis screen against GRCz11. What VEP settings should I use?

polyphen.note: Human-only resource — unavailable for this species

cadd.note: Human-only resource — unavailable for this species

clinvar.note: Human-only resource — unavailable for this species

af_gnomade.note: Human-only resource — unavailable for this species

af_gnomadg.note: Human-only resource — unavailable for this species

justification: Zebrafish has Ensembl variation data and SIFT; use species-agnostic annotation (symbol, biotype, HGVS, exon/intron numbers, SIFT) and withhold all human-only resources.

user_query: Rat (Rattus norvegicus) WGS variants from a knockout model. Which VEP options apply?

polyphen.note: Human-only resource — unavailable for this species

cadd.note: Human-only resource — unavailable for this species

clinvar.note: Human-only resource — unavailable for this species

af_gnomade.note: Human-only resource — unavailable for this species

af_gnomadg.note: Human-only resource — unavailable for this species

justification: Rat has Ensembl variation data and SIFT; use species-agnostic annotation and withhold human-only resources (PolyPhen, CADD, ClinVar, gnomAD).

user_query: I have a short list of rsIDs and just want their gene, consequence, and ClinVar significance - nothing heavy.

sift.note: Not needed for a known-variant lookup

af_gnomade.note: Frequency not needed for identity lookup

justification: A batch rsID lookup needs only gene symbol, co-located known-variant resolution, ClinVar significance, and HGVS; predictors/frequency/regulatory are unnecessary overhead.
